notes: Miami carries a record ~$99.2M dead cap hit from Tua Tagovailoa's post-June 1 release, split across 2026 (~$67.4M) and 2027 (~$31.8M). Additional dead money from Tyreek Hill ($28.2M), Jalen Ramsey ($20.8M), Terron Armstead ($10.7M), and others. Total 2026 dead cap exceeds $175M, severely constraining FA spending. Nearly all signings are 1-year deals. This is a confirmed Year 1 rebuild under new HC Jeff Hafley and new GM Jon-Eric Sullivan.

key_departures.contract_impact: Post-June 1 release; $99.2M dead cap split over 2026 ($67.4M) and 2027 ($31.8M) — NFL record dead cap charge
key_departures.impact: Was benched in 2025 after career-high 15 interceptions. Release was inevitable under new regime. Creates massive QB need but also frees roster from a declining starter on a crippling contract. Dead cap severely limits 2026 flexibility.

key_departures.new_team: unsigned/TBD (rehabbing torn ACL/knee ligaments from Week 4 2025)
key_departures.contract_impact: Cut pre-FA; $28.2M dead cap in 2026, saves $22.9M vs carrying the contract
key_departures.impact: Eight-time Pro Bowler, five-time All-Pro lost to injury in Week 4 2025. His release strips Miami of its primary X-factor and only true elite WR. Jaylen Waddle becomes the undisputed WR1 with no proven WR2 on roster. Massive hole created.

key_departures.contract_impact: Post-June 1 release; saves ~$7.3M in 2026 cap space
key_departures.impact: Led team with 8.5 sacks in 2025 despite being the only credible edge rusher after Jaelan Phillips was traded to Eagles at the deadline. Cut for cap reasons; signed immediately with division rival Bills. EDGE room goes from thin to barren.

key_departures.new_team: New York Jets (traded; 3yr/$40M extension with NYJ)
key_departures.contract_impact: Trade; received 2026 7th-round pick in return. Saved ~$15.6M in 2026 base salary.
key_departures.impact: Traded to division rival Jets for minimal return (7th-round pick). Was still playing at a high level as a versatile nickel safety. Creates a starting-caliber safety void. An intra-division trade that strengthens a conference rival.
Starting guard released in cap purge. Interior OL already thin; losing a starting-caliber guard escalates the IOL need.

key_departures.contract_impact: Non-tendered RFA; signed 3yr deal with NE worth up to $18M
key_departures.impact: Primary blocking TE and rotational pass-catcher non-tendered as Miami declined to match the value. Leaves a gap in the blocking TE role. Greg Dulcich is re-signed but is a pass-catcher, not a blocker.

key_departures.new_team: Carolina Panthers (4yr deal — traded to Eagles at 2025 deadline, signed with Panthers in FA)
key_departures.contract_impact: Traded to Eagles at 2025 trade deadline for a 2026 3rd-round pick (already completed); Phillips then signed with Panthers in 2026 FA
key_departures.impact: Former first-round pick who was traded mid-2025 season. His free agency exit to Carolina is a residual loss — Miami already replaced his cap spot but not his production. Another former pass rusher gone.
Impressed in 2025 season with Miami. Departure creates a punter vacancy.
Eight-year Dolphins kicker released. Creates kicker competition but Riley Patterson was already re-signed.
Rotational linebacker and special teams contributor. Depth loss.
Depth safety and core special teamer. Minor loss given Fitzpatrick's departure creates a much bigger void.
Backup IOL who saw some starts. Departure thins OL depth further.
Four-year team captain, blocking fullback. Departure eliminates the FB role from Miami's offense — consistent with spread/RPO scheme under Hafley.
Rotational WR released in cap purge. Marginal contributor.
Long snapper vacancy now filled by Tucker Addington.

key_departures.details: Traded for 1st (#30), 3rd (#94), 4th (#130). Sent 4th (#111) back with Waddle.
key_departures.impact: Massive loss. Waddle was WR1 and the offense's most dynamic playmaker. WR becomes the most urgent need alongside CB. Draft capital acquired gives Miami flexibility to address multiple holes.

contract.structure_notes: 2026 and 2027 salaries fully guaranteed; includes $22.25M signing bonus; two void years added to spread signing bonus proration over 5 years; deal includes $2M roster bonus if on roster in March 2028; 2026 salary cap hit front-loaded low to help cap situation

signings.analysis: Three-year, $67.5M deal with $45M guaranteed over first two years makes Willis the unquestioned starter for 2026 and 2027 at minimum. AAV of $22.5M ranks 17th among QBs — well below elite tier but well above bridge money. Two years of full guarantees signal genuine commitment, not a stopgap. Willis was connected to new HC Hafley (former GB DC) and GM Sullivan. He went from backup to full-time starter; this is Miami's QB. QB need fully addressed for the near term. They may still draft a developmental QB but it is not a premium Day 1 pick need.

contract.structure_notes: $1.15M base salary (fully guaranteed) + $1.77M signing bonus + up to $340K per-game roster bonuses; cap hit $3.11M; per OTC

signings.analysis: Dulcich had a strong finish to 2025 — 26 catches, 336 yards, 1 TD in just 10 games, leading all TEs in YAC (7.6) and Y/Target (10.2). Re-signed on a short, fully guaranteed deal that locks him as TE1. However, this is a 1-year, $3.1M deal — below starter TE market ($8-14M AAV range). It keeps Miami's best receiving TE on the roster but doesn't solve the long-term TE need, and Julian Hill's departure leaves no quality blocking TE behind him. TE remains a draft priority.

signings.previous_team: Minnesota Vikings (via Green Bay Packers waiver claim)

contract.structure_notes: Reported as 1-year deal; financial terms not disclosed; estimated near minimum

signings.analysis: Signed to replace blocking TE Julian Hill (who went to the Patriots). Sims is a career blocker with only 11 career receptions in 45 games. He fills the blocking TE roster spot but adds no pass-catching threat. Minimum-caliber deal — does not address TE as a true offensive weapon need. Miami still needs a receiving TE upgrade via the draft.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; exact terms not fully disclosed; estimated per market context

signings.analysis: 1,535 career receiving yards and 5 TDs in five NFL seasons. Speed threat (4.32 40) who can beat defenses deep. However, ESPN's own tracker notes he is 'not likely the No. 2 receiver Miami still needs opposite Jaylen Waddle.' 1-year depth deal — does not resolve the WR2 void left by Tyreek Hill. Miami still urgently needs a true WR2 via draft or FA.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; monetary terms not disclosed by reporters; estimated per market/roster context

signings.analysis: 6-1, 195 lbs; brings size Atwell lacks. Has pre-existing rapport with Malik Willis from the Senior Bowl. 91 catches, 1,093 yards, 10 TDs in four Cowboys seasons — flashed in 2024 but fell behind the depth chart in 2025. At 27 on a 1-year prove-it deal, he may compete for WR2 reps but is not a true WR2 solution. WR need partially addressed in depth only — still a critical need for the draft.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; monetary terms not publicly reported; estimated near minimum based on reporting ('likely minimum-value contract')

signings.analysis: Had 11.5 sacks in 2022 breakout but only 10 total sacks in the other five seasons. In 2025 with Eagles, generated 23 pressures on 132 snaps (17.4% pressure rate) but only 1 sack. Rotational piece only — undersized at 6-2, 226 lbs. 1-year near-minimum deal. Provides pass-rush depth alongside Chop Robinson but does not fill Chubb's starter role. EDGE remains a top-tier draft need.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; no monetary terms reported; estimated near minimum

signings.analysis: Former Ravens 2nd-round pick who has struggled with repeated injuries throughout his career. 4.5 career sacks in 32 games. Has special teams experience (career-high 69 ST snaps in 2025). Could be a camp body or rotational project. Does not meaningfully address EDGE starter need — upside play on a talent that has never stayed healthy.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; terms not disclosed

signings.analysis: Former 5th-round 49ers pick; 1 sack in 3 NFL seasons, primarily rotational down lineman. Projects as depth with ST contributions. 14 tackles in 7 games in 2025. Low-cost addition that adds depth but does not address the starter void at EDGE. Need unchanged.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; exact terms not publicly reported; estimated based on market context and beat reporter framing as a starter-level addition

signings.analysis: Started 40 games over 4 seasons with the Chargers at both LT (19) and RG (21). ESPN notes he 'spent a vast majority of his time at right guard — nearly 1,500 snaps' and Miami 'may have just found their new starter' at RG. Versatile enough for multiple spots. However, this is a 1-year deal for a player with a mediocre PFF grade (68.2 pass block, 61.5 run block in 2025). With James Daniels released and Cole Strange gone, IOL remains a need — Salyer partially addresses it for 2026 but Miami still needs IOL depth and long-term solutions.

signings.previous_team: Miami Dolphins (re-sign; was on Rams PS before joining MIA mid-2025)

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; terms not disclosed

signings.analysis: 6-2, 198 lbs — great size for a corner. Coach Hafley has extensive DB coaching background. Does not have significant starting experience at this level. Re-signed as a developmental piece under a CB-specialist HC. Does not address the CB1/CB2 starter need. CB remains a critical draft need.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; terms not disclosed

signings.analysis: Started 19 games over the past two seasons with the Titans. 6-1, 190 lbs. Could compete for a starting spot depending on how the CB room develops under Hafley. Upside project on a minimum deal. Does not resolve CB starter need given the lack of established outside CBs on the roster.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; no monetary terms reported

signings.analysis: 224 career tackles, 17 PDs, 4 INTs in 92 career games. Primarily played special teams with Raiders in 2025 after being activated in Week 10 from IR. Versatile DB who can play safety or nickel corner. Minimum deal — does not address the Fitzpatrick starter void at safety. S remains a significant need.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; no monetary terms reported; RFA non-tendered by GB

signings.analysis: Followed new HC Hafley and new GM Sullivan from Green Bay to Miami — scheme familiarity. Active in 16 of 17 games in 2025 before ankle injury in finale. Primarily a ST contributor and depth piece. Minimum-level signing; does not resolve the Fitzpatrick starter-level gap. S need remains.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; no monetary terms reported

signings.analysis: 29 career games (6 starts) with 31 tackles, 8 PDs, 1 INT. RFA non-tendered by Patriots. Special teams contributor who could compete for depth CB role. Does not address CB starter need.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; terms not reported

signings.analysis: Florida native (Fort Lauderdale) and UF alum; former Cardinals 4th-round pick (2021). Last starting experience was 2023. ESPN notes he has starting experience 'albeit not since 2023' and 'will likely compete for a depth spot, but all bets are off until Miami adds a bonafide starting corner.' One of four CBs signed, all on depth/minimum deals. CB starter need unchanged.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; exact terms not reported; framed as inexpensive re-signing

signings.analysis: 2-time Super Bowl champion with the Chiefs. Participated in all 17 games in 2025, recording 20 tackles and 2 sacks. Training camp standout but limited in-game role (131 defensive snaps). Re-signed for depth and ST value. LB was not a critical pre-FA need.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; terms not reported; framed as inexpensive re-signing

signings.analysis: 12 tackles in 12 games in 2025 with one start. Re-signed for depth and continuity at DT alongside Zach Sieler. Rotational piece with ST utility. IDL was not a top pre-FA need given Sieler remains on the roster.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal per reports

signings.analysis: 22-of-23 on 40-49 yard FGs over past 3 seasons. Miami released Jason Sanders and is setting up a kicking competition between Gonzalez and re-signed Riley Patterson. K need addressed for 2026.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal

signings.analysis: Went 27-of-29 on FGs in 2025 (93.1%), setting a single-season franchise record for FG percentage. Re-signed to compete with Gonzalez. One of them wins the job.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; rookie-level contract

signings.analysis: Has never punted in an NFL game; averaged 45.4 yards per punt in the UFL in 2025. Replaces Jake Bailey (who signed with Falcons). Unproven at NFL level — team may add competition. P need partially addressed but remains uncertain.

contract.structure_notes: 1-year deal; standard LS contract

signings.analysis: Replaces Joe Cardona (who went to Rams). LS need addressed — routine roster spot filled.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Willis signing (3yr/$67.5M, $45M gtd) fully addresses starter QB. Two guaranteed years signal genuine investment, not a bridge. Miami may still want a developmental QB later in the draft (Day 3), but QB is no longer a top-priority pick. Could still select a long-term project in Rounds 3-5 given Willis turns 26 and this is a rebuild.

Tyreek Hill's release (injury/cap) removes the WR1 opposite Waddle. Atwell (1yr depth) and Tolbert (1yr prove-it) are both one-year fliers that don't solve the WR2 problem. ESPN explicitly noted Atwell is 'not likely the No. 2 receiver Miami still needs.' WR escalates to Tier 1 critical need — Miami almost certainly takes WR in Round 1 or early Round 2.

Chubb released (cap), Phillips already traded at 2025 deadline. EDGE room now consists of Chop Robinson (limited starter), Josh Uche (1yr rotational, near minimum), David Ojabo (injury-plagued camp body), and Robert Beal Jr. (depth). None of the three new EDGE additions are on multi-year deals or starter-caliber contracts. EDGE is a Tier 1 need alongside WR heading into the draft.

Miami signed four CBs in free agency (Baker, Austin, Wilson, re-signed AJ Green III) — but all are on minimum/depth 1-year deals with no proven starter among them. ESPN noted 'the Dolphins cornerback room is barren' and Wilson 'will likely compete for a depth spot.' No CB1 or CB2 has been established. CB remains a Tier 1 draft need — perhaps the most pressing defensive need.

Minkah Fitzpatrick was traded to Jets for a mere 7th-round pick. Elijah Campbell departed to Giants. The only 'additions' at safety are Zayne Anderson (depth/ST, minimum) and Lonnie Johnson (depth/ST). Neither is a starting-caliber free safety/strong safety. Safety becomes a Tier 1 need — Miami may target both S and CB aggressively in the draft.

James Daniels was released, Cole Strange left to Chargers, Liam Eichenberg released on failed physical. Jamaree Salyer (1yr) is a potential starting RG who brings positional versatility. However, it's a 1-year, depth-market deal — not a long-term solution. IOL remains a Tier 2 need. Miami should still draft an interior lineman to develop alongside Salyer and young 2025 pick Jonah Savaiinaea.

Julian Hill non-tendered (went to Patriots for 3yr/$18M — Miami passed). Dulcich re-signed (1yr/$3.1M — good pass-catcher but no blocking TE). Sims added as blocking TE minimum (11 career receptions). TE room has a pass-catcher in Dulcich but lacks a true dual-threat or quality blocking TE. TE escalates slightly to Tier 2 — worth targeting in Rounds 3-4 in the draft.

Austin Jackson restructured ($7M/1yr, $5.475M gtd) to remain as the RT. Patrick Paul and Carter Warren remain as developmental LTs. Larry Borom and Kendall Lamm departed. The OT situation is thin and unresolved long-term, but Jackson's restructure stabilizes the RT spot for 2026. OT remains a Tier 3 need — worth addressing in the middle rounds of the draft.

key_additions.headline: New-regime franchise QB replaces Tua; $45M guaranteed over 2 years signals real investment in a rebuild starter
40-career-start OL veteran joins as projected starting RG, addressing the biggest void left by James Daniels' release
Miami native returns home on a prove-it deal; best pass rush option on roster after Chubb's departure but far from a solution
Re-signed pass-catching TE who led all TEs in YAC and yards-per-target in 2025; expected to be focal point of Willis-led passing game
Miami native and deep-ball threat adds speed to WR room but does not solve the WR2 problem opposite Waddle
Size (6-1) and Willis familiarity (Senior Bowl connection) make Tolbert the top WR2 candidate on a depth prove-it deal
Followed Hafley and Sullivan from GB to MIA; brings scheme familiarity but is a depth/ST player, not Fitzpatrick's replacement

key_losses.headline: Traded WR1 for major draft capital — 1st (#30), 3rd (#94), 4th (#130). WR is now the roster's most glaring hole.
Franchise QB era ends after benching; NFL-record $99.2M dead cap hit defines Miami's 2026-27 cap picture
Eight-time Pro Bowler released after Week 4 knee injury; leaves a WR1-opposite-Waddle void that Miami has not filled
Team's best pass rusher (8.5 sacks in 2025) cut for cap reasons, then immediately signed by division rival Bills

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to NYJ for 2026 7th-round pick; signed 3yr/$40M with NYJ
key_losses.headline: Elite safety traded to division rival for minimum return; creates unaddressed starting safety void

key_losses.contract_summary: Non-tendered RFA; signed 3yr/up to $18M with Patriots
key_losses.headline: Primary blocking TE non-tendered and lost to division rival; highlights Miami's rebuild-mode roster philosophy
Starting guard released in cap purge, deepening interior OL need heading into the draft

notes_on_confidence: All signings confirmed via official Miami Dolphins team announcements, ESPN tracker, NFL.com, and credible beat reporters (Barry Jackson/Miami Herald, Tom Pelissero/NFL Network, Adam Schefter/ESPN, Jordan Schultz). Contract figures for Willis from Tom Pelissero/MMQB's Albert Breer. Dulcich contract from OverTheCap. Some depth-signing contract values (Uche, Tolbert, Atwell, Baker, Anderson, Johnson, Austin, Wilson, Beal, Ojabo, Salyer) not publicly disclosed and are estimated based on market context, cap constraints, and reporting framing. All are 1-year deals. Willis, Dulcich, and Gonzalez figures are confirmed.
